pr.title: Group the wiki TU list into progress sections
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Updated tools/wiki_tu.py so the generated Translation Units wiki page is no longer one flat table. The script now buckets TUs into `.text in progress`, `.text matched - data / linking left`, and `Fully linked`, adds separate `.text %` and `data %` columns, removes the old combined percent/checkmark status column, preserves assignee round-tripping, and tidies size and Discord-handle formatting. This was a tooling/documentation ergonomics PR rather than a code matching PR.

key_files.role: Only changed file; rewrote the wiki TU table emission logic into helpers and progress buckets.

what_changed: Replaced the single flat Translation Units markdown table with three generated sections: `.text in progress`, `.text matched - data / linking left`, and `Fully linked`.

Classified units using `metadata.complete` as the canonical fully-linked/done flag; otherwise TUs with `matched_code_percent >= 100` go into the data/linking-left bucket, and the rest go into the in-progress bucket.
Added separate `.text %` and `data %` table columns so code-matched units with remaining data work are distinguishable from completed units.
Removed the old combined `%` plus heavy-check/cross linked-status column; section membership now communicates progress state.
Kept assignee columns in the active tables so `read_wiki` can continue round-tripping Discord and GitHub assignees.
Moved repeated formatting into helpers: `friendly_size`, `percent_cell`, `file_of`, `assignee_cells`, `percent_cells`, `row_full`, and `row_slim`.
Formatted generated sizes with ` ` instead of spaces after `humanfriendly.format_size`, preventing values like `1.18 KB` from wrapping inside table cells.
Normalized Discord assignees to exactly one leading `@` by emitting `@{assignee['discord'].lstrip('@')}`.
Printed section counts on the line below each header instead of embedding counts in headers, keeping markdown header anchor URLs stable.
smart_moves: Used `metadata.complete` as the authoritative finished-unit signal because it drives the official completed-unit count, avoiding confusion between code matching and fully linked completion.
Separated `.text %` from `data %`, which makes the remaining work visible when code is already 100% but data or linking is not done.
Kept section header text stable while still showing counts, preventing wiki anchor links from changing whenever progress numbers change.
Preserved assignee column positions in the active tables to avoid breaking the existing `read_wiki` parsing/round-trip behavior.
Introduced small row/formatting helpers rather than expanding the original monolithic loop, making future wiki table changes easier and less error-prone.
Used an em dash for percent cells when the total for a section type is zero, avoiding misleading 0% or divide-by-zero behavior.
decomp_lessons: For project progress pages, separate code matching progress from data/linking completion; a 100% `.text` TU is not necessarily a finished TU.
When generating wiki sections that people link to, do not include volatile counts or emoji in the markdown header text; put counts below the header to keep anchors stable.
When maintaining editable generated wiki pages, preserve parser-sensitive table shape and column indices unless the reader/parser is updated at the same time.
Use the existing completion metadata as the canonical source of truth for fully linked/decomp-complete status rather than inferring completion from a single percentage.
Generated assignment fields should normalize user input on write, allowing contributors to enter simple plaintext while keeping the rendered wiki consistent.
naming_conventions: Progress section names used in the generated wiki: `.text in progress`, `.text matched - data / linking left`, and `Fully linked`.
Column naming convention changed from a generic `%` status column to explicit `.text %` and `data %` columns.
Helper names are direct and domain-specific: `friendly_size`, `percent_cell`, `file_of`, `assignee_cells`, `percent_cells`, `row_full`, and `row_slim`.
Discord assignees are rendered with exactly one leading `@` regardless of how they were entered or parsed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactic was changed in this PR.
The relevant decomp-progress tactic is reporting `.text` matching separately from data matching and final linking, so contributors can identify what kind of work remains for each translation unit.

review_feedback.feedback: Remove emoji from headers because they do not render in the table of contents.
Place counts below each section header so section hyperlink URLs remain consistent.
Do not use a `details` tag; the linked TUs are already at the bottom of the page and the tables should be fine for browsers/devices to render.
Replace spaces in humanfriendly size strings with ` `.
Discord usernames should start with exactly one `@`, whether or not one existed in the parsed value.

follow_up_queries: Find later changes to tools/wiki_tu.py that modify read_wiki parsing or assignee column positions.
Search for future PRs that adjust metadata.complete semantics or the official completed-unit count.
Check whether generated Translation Units wiki sections remained stable after this PR.
Look for follow-up tooling that consumes `.text %` and `data %` separately.
